sections.paragraph:
ἀκατακάλυπτος, ον (Polyb. 15, 27, 2; Lev 13:45; AcPhil 60 [Aa II/2, 25, 13]) uncovered ἀ. τῇ κεφαλῇ (Philo, Spec. Leg. 3, 60) with unc. head (of praying women; cp. SIG 999, 10 and 736, 22; Philostrat. Jun. [III a.d.]: APF 14, ’41, p. 8; 19 ln. 60f: γυναῖκες ἀκάλυπτοι in a solemn procession) 1 Cor 11:5. Concisely γυναῖκα ἀ. a woman without head-covering vs. 13.—JJeremias, TLZ 91,’66,431; JMurphy-O’Connor, CBQ 42, ’80, 482–500. DELG s.v. καλύπτω.

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

Aa Aa = Acta apostolorum apocrypha—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

Philostrat Philostrat = Flavius Philostratus, the Sophist II–III a.d.—List 5

APF APF = Archiv für Papyrusforschung—Lists 4, 6

  1. ln. = line

TLZ TLZ = Theolog. Literaturzeitung—List 6

CBQ CBQ = Catholic Biblical Quarterly—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 35
